name: Whole Wheat Buttermilk Pancakes
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Buttermilk -or- Sour Milk (1-cup lowfat milk + 1-tablespoon vinegar)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, stir together the flours, sugar, baking powder, baking soda, and salt.
2. In another bowl, beat the egg, buttermilk (or sour milk mixture), and oil together until well combined.
3. Pour the liquid mixture into the dry ingredients and stir just until blended, scraping the sides of the bowl to ensure no lumps remain.
4. Heat a non-stick griddle or large skillet over medium heat. Grease lightly with vegetable oil if needed.
5. For each pancake, scoop about 1/4 cup batter onto the hot surface.
6. Cook until bubbles form on the surface and the edges start to look set (about 2-3 minutes).
7. Flip the pancakes and cook the other side until golden brown (about 1-2 minutes more).
8. Serve with fresh fruit slices, unsweetened applesauce, or a low-calorie syrup.
9. Makes approximately 12 servings.
Two Pancakes Nutritional Information:
Calories: 114
Carbohydrates: 18g
Protein: 4g
Fat: 3g
Sodium: 246mg
Potassium: 1200mg
Cholesterol: 47mg
Exchange Value: 1 Bread Exchange + 1/2 Fat Exchange
